description:
You are a deployment engineer about to roll-out an application update.  
    Before proceeding, you must perform a quick baseline network sanity-check on
    the machine and record the results for the change-management system.

    1. Create a directory named
       /home/user/network_diagnostics  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Run each of the following three commands **exactly** as written, in the
       order shown (do not add options, do not omit options):

          a) ping -c 1 127.0.0.1  
          b) getent hosts localhost  
          c) ss -ltn

       • Capture only each command’s final shell exit status  
         (i.e. the value returned by “echo $?” immediately after
         the command finishes).  
       • Ignore all other text that the command prints.

    3. Create a plain-text log file called
       /home/user/network_diagnostics/diag.log  
       containing **exactly four lines**, terminated with a single newline at
       the end of the last line.  No blank lines, no extra spaces.

       Line-by-line format (all lower-case, no spaces):

          1) # Network Diagnostics Report
          2) ping_exit_code=<numeric_status_of_step_2a>
          3) getent_exit_code=<numeric_status_of_step_2b>
          4) ss_exit_code=<numeric_status_of_step_2c>

       Example of a perfectly formatted file when all three commands succeed:

          # Network Diagnostics Report
          ping_exit_code=0
          getent_exit_code=0
          ss_exit_code=0

       Your log file **must** match the required four-line pattern exactly, so
       that the automated verifier can do a strict comparison.

    4. Do not output anything else to the terminal other than what the commands
       naturally print; the validator only checks the existence and contents of
       /home/user/network_diagnostics/diag.log.

    Goal: when you finish, the file
    /home/user/network_diagnostics/diag.log will exist and conform exactly to
    the four-line format described above.
truth:
Expected final artefact:

    Path: /home/user/network_diagnostics/diag.log  
    Permissions: 0644 (agent’s default umask is acceptable)  
    Contents (including the trailing newline):

    # Network Diagnostics Report
    ping_exit_code=0
    getent_exit_code=0
    ss_exit_code=0

    The automated tests will:

      • Verify that /home/user/network_diagnostics exists and is a directory.  
      • Verify that diag.log exists inside that directory.  
      • Read diag.log and require an exact byte-for-byte match with the four
        lines listed above (Unix LF line endings), including the single trailing
        newline at the end of the file.
